steps.title: Set up
steps.content: One acute angle measures $9x - 650$. Each obtuse angle is its supplement: $180 - (9x - 650)$. The sum of one acute and three obtuse angles equals $w$:$$w = (9x-650) + 3[180-(9x-650)]$$

steps.title: Solve
steps.content: Expand: $w = (9x-650) + 540 - 3(9x-650) = -2(9x-650) + 540 = -18x + 1300 + 540 = -18x + 1840$. The $x$ terms cancel when you substitute back into $w$, giving $w = 1840$.
